title: WAF
description: Web Application Firewall that protects against common web exploits — SQL injection, XSS, bot traffic, and DDoS. Attaches to ALB, CloudFront, or API Gateway as a shield layer.
practices: Start with AWS Managed Rule Groups (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, SQLiRuleSet) before writing custom rules.
Use rate-based rules to protect against brute force and DDoS — set limits based on your normal traffic patterns.
Attach WAF to CloudFront for global protection, or to ALB/APIGW for regional protection.
Enable logging to S3 or CloudWatch to understand what WAF is blocking — false positives are common initially.
configNotes: Scope: REGIONAL (for ALB, API Gateway) or CLOUDFRONT (for CloudFront distributions).
Managed rule groups provide pre-built protection against common attacks.
Default action (allow or block) determines what happens to requests that don't match any rule.
